CheckTitle: Security group restricts Cassandra access from the internet
Description: Checks that Cassandra TCP ports 7199, 9160, and 8888 are not exposed to unrestricted IPv4 (`0.0.0.0/0`) or IPv6 (`::/0`) sources.
Risk: Public Cassandra management and client access enables unauthorized administration, credential attacks, and data compromise.

Code.CLI:
aliyun ecs RevokeSecurityGroup --RegionId <region_id> --SecurityGroupId <security_group_id> --IpProtocol tcp --PortRange 7199/7199 --SourceCidrIp 0.0.0.0/0
aliyun ecs RevokeSecurityGroup --RegionId <region_id> --SecurityGroupId <security_group_id> --IpProtocol tcp --PortRange 7199/7199 --Ipv6SourceCidrIp ::/0
aliyun ecs RevokeSecurityGroup --RegionId <region_id> --SecurityGroupId <security_group_id> --IpProtocol tcp --PortRange 9160/9160 --SourceCidrIp 0.0.0.0/0
aliyun ecs RevokeSecurityGroup --RegionId <region_id> --SecurityGroupId <security_group_id> --IpProtocol tcp --PortRange 9160/9160 --Ipv6SourceCidrIp ::/0
aliyun ecs RevokeSecurityGroup --RegionId <region_id> --SecurityGroupId <security_group_id> --IpProtocol tcp --PortRange 8888/8888 --SourceCidrIp 0.0.0.0/0
aliyun ecs RevokeSecurityGroup --RegionId <region_id> --SecurityGroupId <security_group_id> --IpProtocol tcp --PortRange 8888/8888 --Ipv6SourceCidrIp ::/0
Code.Other: Restrict Cassandra ingress for both `SourceCidrIp` and `Ipv6SourceCidrIp` to cluster members, application hosts, or trusted CIDRs.

Recommendation.Text: Do not expose TCP ports 7199, 9160, or 8888 to `0.0.0.0/0` or `::/0`.
